setups.name: The Renter's Starter Kit
setups.narrator: The cheapest way to make a rented room feel like yours — nothing that needs a drill, nothing your landlord will notice when you leave.

items.note: No drilling — turns unused door space into actual storage.

items.product: Command Picture Hanging Extra Large Strips (Set of 10)
items.note: Holds up to 7.2kg — the honest way to hang art without losing your deposit.
The cheapest single swap that actually changes how a room feels.
Collapse flat when not needed — useful for a tenancy that might end.

sources: Cross-referenced UK retail listings, July 2026 — see category guides for detail.

setups.name: The Cosy Corner
setups.narrator: Layering, not renovating — the small stuff that actually makes a room feel warm.

items.note: No electrician needed — plugs straight into an existing socket.

items.product: IKEA TIPHEDE or KNARDRUP small rug
items.note: Big enough to anchor the seating area — a rug that 'floats' between furniture makes a room look smaller, not bigger.
Texture over pattern — the layering trick most 2026 living-room guides lean on.
Three to five cushions is the sweet spot before it starts eating seating space.

sources: Cross-referenced UK retail listings, July 2026 — see category guides for detail.

setups.name: The Storage-Strapped Studio
setups.narrator: For a room that has to do more than one job — storage that doesn't eat floor space.

items.note: Doubles as seating, coffee table, and storage in one — the best pound-for-pound multi-use buy.
Adhesive-mounted — no bracket, no drilling. Lightweight items only.

items.note: Flat, sturdy, no wasted bulk — 120cm fits most UK box rooms.

Doubles as task lighting for the desk corner — no electrician needed.
